reasoning: The Lean statement fixes an arbitrary z on the unit circle (via ‖z‖ = 1) and defines s n as the partial sum ∑_{i<n} i·z^i, which is the n-th partial sum of the series ∑ n z^n (the i=0 term vanishes). The conclusion ¬ ∃ y, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 y) expresses that the partial sums have no limit, i.e., the series does not converge.
